item.title: Glimpses of a Vim configuration
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
It's been a while since I start짯ed track짯ing ver짯sions of my cus짯tom Vim con짯fig짯u짯ra짯tion, and mak짯ing it avail짯able as an open source soft짯ware in Github. The best of this project is, in my opin짯ion, to have it un짯der ver짯sion con짯trol, so I can track changes and re짯leas짯es.

Ev짯ery once in a while, when I find a new set짯ting, or a great new fea짯ture, I mod짯i짯fy the con짯fig짯u짯ra짯tion, so they will be짯come avail짯able on the next re짯lease. Be짯sides the fea짯tures that are men짯tioned in the pro짯jec짯t, and the cus짯tomiza짯tions made, I feel very com짯fort짯able with the colour scheme I made.

Here are some glimpses of it:

First cap짯ture of colours, and lay짯out

The colour scheme is gen짯er짯al for the syn짯tax high짯light짯ing of all types rec짯og짯nized by Vim. Please note this might al짯so de짯pend on the con짯fig짯u짯ra짯tion of your ter짯mi짯nal.

The tabs are al짯so themed ac짯cord짯ing to the menus.

Any sug짯ges짯tions or im짯prove짯ments to the code and con짯fig짯u짯ra짯tion can be made on the Github projec짯t.

item.title: Alpine email client
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Alpine is a soft짯ware I def짯i짯nite짯ly rec짯om짯mend for those who like min짯i짯mal짯is짯tic ap짯pli짯ca짯tion짯s that go straight to the point in or짯der to get the things done. It's an email clien짯t, with no GUI, which al짯lows me to read emails bet짯ter than by means of the web page [1] with짯out any dis짯trac짯tion짯s, fo짯cus짯ing on what is just im짯por짯tan짯t. It is al짯so great that shows on짯ly the text (the rel짯e짯vant part of the email) and it can fol짯low the links by launch짯ing the de짯fault brows짯er.

It is sim짯ple, fast and per짯fect짯ly suit짯able for check짯ing my old in짯box with week짯ly lists up짯dates. It's in짯her짯it짯ed from an old pro짯jec짯t, called pine, and in Fe짯do짯ra it can be in짯stalled by:

sudo dnf install alpine

Then you just need to con짯fig짯ure the email ac짯count set짯tings (one last time to vis짯it the page, but that would be the last :-) and you're ready.

[1]Not for gmail but from another older account I have

item.title: Presentations with reveal.js
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya / @rmarianoa
item.description:
Old-짯fash짯ioned PP짯T's pre짯sen짯ta짯tions are from the 90's, and let's face it, we are in the age of the web brows짯er. So, the last time I had to gave a talk, I de짯cid짯ed to use a bet짯ter tech짯ni짯cal sup짯port.

Af짯ter a quick search for al짯ter짯na짯tives, I found many op짯tion짯s, in짯clud짯ing well-짯known li짯braries, un짯til I fi짯nal짯ly de짯cid짯ed for re짯veal.js.

It is writ짯ten in JavaScript with good CSS themes, and it does not re짯quire ex짯pert knowl짯edge on those tech짯nolo짯gies. In or짯der to play the pre짯sen짯ta짯tion, you launch an HTML file from a web brows짯er or y짯ou can al짯so run it with a stat짯ic serv짯er.

Ad짯van짯tages:

  • Ver짯sion con짯trol: Giv짯en the fact that your pre짯sen짯ta짯tion is made from source code, it is pos짯si짯ble 짯to track changes by us짯ing git.
  • A bet짯ter cross-짯plat짯form sup짯port: it does not re짯ly on a par짯tic짯u짯lar soft짯ware in a par짯tic짯u짯lar ver짯sion to be present (web browsers are ubiq짯ui짯tous nowa짯days).
  • Com짯pat짯i짯bil짯i짯ty: WYSI짯WYG.
  • Able to host your pre짯sen짯ta짯tion in the cloud and ac짯cess it from any짯where.

Rea짯sons to use it / nice things about it:

  • We are in 2014
  • Sup짯ports Mark짯down lan짯guage

To be clear: I am not say짯ing that this is a bet짯ter al짯ter짯na짯tive be짯cause is new짯er of mod짯ern, but be짯cause of the ad짯van짯tages list짯ed. In oth짯er word짯s, if we count with de짯vel짯oped tool짯s at our dis짯pos짯al, it would be a good idea to use them.

Here are some sim짯ple and ba짯sic ex짯am짯ples of pre짯sen짯ta짯tions I am work짯ing on. (DIS짯CLAIMER: they might be in dif짯fer짯ent lan짯guages, and the page is in pro짯gress).
